title: Volatile Adsorption Sensing Prototype (VASP): Chemometric Characterization of Gaseous Metabolites at the Edge
author: Vivas, Daniel
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The diagnosis of systemic pathologies through Volatile Organic Compounds (VOCs) represents a low-cost alternative to traditional clinical labs. This work presents the VASP (Volatile Adsorption Sensing Prototype) module. Utilizing MOX sensors on an ESP32-S3, the system captures chemometric signatures from biological headspaces (breath, blood, urine) without chemical reagents. We detail the mass transfer foundations based on Henry’s Law, the edge computing logic for real-time inference, and the clinical correlates of detectable VOCs. This reagent-free tool aims to provide diagnostic sovereignty in resource constrained environments.
